photos.title: BombShelter Abri PlaceDeLaMadeleine Geneva RomanDeckert17032021.jpg
photos.description: Relief sculpture of a bomb falling between the Île Rousseau and the Saint-Pierre cathedral in Geneva, Switzerland, above the entrance door to a former bunker at Place de la Madeleine in the Old Town, constructed at the beginning of the Second World War to provide shelter to some 1,200 people. It was converted in the 2010s to a cultural space run by the Fondation L'Abri.
photos.author: RomanDeckert

photos.title: BombRelief Shelter Abri PlaceDeLaMadeleine Geneva RomanDeckert15032021.jpg
photos.description: Relief sculpture of a bomb falling between the Île Rousseau and the Saint-Pierre cathedral in Geneva, Switzerland, above the entrance door to a former bunker at Place de la Madeleine in the Old Town, constructed at the beginning of the Second World War to provide shelter to some 1,200 people. It was converted in the 2010s to a cultural space run by the Fondation L'Abri.

photos.title: Phallus sur andouiller la Madeleine.jpg
photos.description: Bois de cervidé, andouiller, gravé de manière à figurer un phallus. Abri de la Madeleine, Tursac, Dordogne, v. 19000-14000 AP. Musée national de la Préhistoire, Les Eyzies.
photos.author: Zunkir

photos.title: Propulseur hyène rampante - La Madeleine - Tursac - MNP.png
photos.description: Atlatl "creeping hyena", found in La Madeleine rock shelter in Tursac, Dordogne, Aquitaine, France. 10.7 cm. Magdalenian culture. It can be viewed in the National Prehistory Museum in Les Eyzies-de-Tayac.
photos.author:
• Klaus D. Peter, Wiehl, Germany
  • Derivative work: Sémhur (talk)
Sémhur
